name: Neapolitan-Style Crosti
recipeIngredient: 16 slices of Italian bread, cut 1/2-inch thick
1/3 cup black olivada OR 1/3 cup (4 oz.) Calamata olives, pitted or finely chopped
1/2 lb fresh mozzarella cheese, cut into 16 slices
1/2 lb plum tomatoes, cut lengthwise into 16 slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F. Brush one side of each bread slice with some olive oil and arrange on a baking sheet (oiled side up). Bake until lightly browned and crusty, about 4 minutes.
2. Spread olivada or chopped olives on each toast. Cover each with a slice of mozzarella and top with a slice of tomato. Brush tomatoes with remaining oil and sprinkle with salt and pepper. Bake until cheese is melted, about 8 to 10 minutes. Serve immediately.
Olivada is a puree of black or green olives packed in oil available at specialty food stores. You can substitute tapenade or make your own. (Note: I plan to use pesto from the refrigerator instead of olivada and Creole or homegrown tomatoes.)
